message.content:
You are an independent fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for a production-hardening pass on the hc-assay repo at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. Be adversarial and skeptical — your job is to find any way the fix is wrong, incomplete, or its regression test fails to discriminate.

FINDING J-003 (MED):
CI editable install fetched the hatchling build backend LIVE and un-hashed from PyPI (an unscanned, non-reproducible build-time code-execution window) because builds used default build isolation. Fix: new requirements-build.lock (hashed hatchling/editables/transitive); CI installs it with --require-hashes then "pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e ." in all three lanes (core, integration, license-gate); a reproducibility-guard diff regenerates and compares requirements-build.lock. In .github/workflows/ci.yml + requirements-build.lock. Tests: test_ci_pins_build_backend_and_disables_live_build_isolation.

The complete code diff for all pass-7 fixes is at /tmp/pass7_code.diff (read it). The full files are under /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. You may run the test suite (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path>) — NOTE: use "python -m pytest", not bare "pytest", or the tests/ package won't import.

Judge ONLY finding J-003. Decide:
- appropriate: does the fix address the real root cause with a sound approach (not a band-aid, not the wrong layer)?
- complete: is there any remaining gap, AND does the regression test actually FAIL if the fix is reverted (i.e. does it discriminate)? If you can, reason about or test revert-discrimination.
Return CONFIRMED only if both are true. Return CONCERN if appropriate but with a real residual gap. Return REJECTED if the fix is wrong or the test is non-discriminating. List concrete concerns.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review finding J-003. Let me start by reading the diff and relevant files.

diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index ed0873a..9e1e25c 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -32,7 +32,8 @@ jobs:
           # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
       - name: Lint (ruff)
         run: ruff check src tests
       - name: Format check (ruff)
@@ -77,7 +78,8 @@ jobs:
           # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
       - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
         run: mypy
       - name: Verify service containers are reachable (no silent skip)
@@ -136,6 +138,12 @@ jobs:
           uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
           diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
             || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
+          # the build-backend lockfile (hatchling etc.) backs the hash-pinned --no-build-isolation
+          # editable install (#J-003) and must stay in sync.
+          printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in
+          uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock
+          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-build.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock) \
+            || { echo "::error::requirements-build.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile <(printf 'hatchling\\neditables\\n') --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-build.lock"; exit 1; }
       - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
         run: |
           set -euo pipefail
@@ -188,9 +196,10 @@ jobs:
           # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
           # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
           python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
-          pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
+          pip-licenses --format=json > licenses.json  # scan the dep set, not system tooling (#J-006)
           python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
       - name: Upload license report
         if: always()
diff --git a/requirements-build.lock b/requirements-build.lock
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..4b1395d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/requirements-build.lock
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@

+packaging==26.2 \

+pluggy==1.6.0 \
+    --hash=sha256:7dcc130b76258d33b90f61b658791dede3486c3e6bfb003ee5c9bfb396dd22f3 \
+    --hash=sha256:e920276dd6813095e9377c0bc5566d94c932c33b27a3e3945d8389c374dd4746

+trove-classifiers==2026.6.1.19 \

+    --hash=sha256:c5132b4b61a829d11cfbd2d72e97f20a45ed6edb95e45c5efdeb5e00836b2745

diff --git a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
index 465013e..fb2ff89 100755
--- a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
+++ b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
@@ -73,7 +73,52 @@ def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
     return re.compile(re.escape(name))
 
 
+def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
+    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
+
+    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
+    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
+    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
+    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
+    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
+    over-redacting common words.
+    """
+    names: list[str] = []
+    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
+    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
+    # Derive the operator name from SEVERAL sources — any one may be empty in a given context
+    # (#J-002 follow-up): on this node local `git config user.name` is unset yet commits still
+    # carry an author via global config / env, so a single-source lookup scrubbed nothing. Cover
+    # the env author vars, git config, AND the actual last-commit author.
+    for env_name in ("GIT_AUTHOR_NAME", "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME"):
+        v = os.environ.get(env_name, "").strip()
+        if v:
+            names.append(v)
+    try:
+        import subprocess
+
+        for cmd in (["git", "config", "user.name"], ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%an"]):
+            out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
+            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
+                names.append(out.stdout.strip())
+    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
+        pass
+    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
+    seen: set[str] = set()
+    for n in names:
+        if len(n) < 4 or n in seen:
+            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
+        seen.add(n)
+        # Flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]"). NO leading \b: the name often
+        # appears glued to a JSON-escaped newline ("...---\\n[REDACTED]") where \b doesn't fire
+        # because the escape's 'n' abuts the name (#J-002 follow-up). A trailing \b still prevents
+        # matching inside a longer word (e.g. "Joneses"); the >=4-char guard bounds over-redaction.
+        pats.append(re.compile(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in n.split()) + r"\b"))
+    return pats
+
+
 _USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
+_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
 
 
 def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
@@ -88,6 +133,10 @@ def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
     # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
     if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
         text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
+    # Redact the operator's full display name too (#J-002) — distinct PII the username/email rules
+    # leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
+    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
+        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
     return text
 
 
@@ -104,13 +153,18 @@ def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
         # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
         # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
         # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
-        # Decode strictly to classify: text → scrub; genuinely binary (rare here) → copy verbatim.
-        try:
-            text = raw.decode("utf-8")
-        except UnicodeDecodeError:
-            data = raw  # binary blob — nothing to scrub
+        #
+        # FAIL CLOSED on redaction (#J-001): classify binary by an actual binary SIGNAL (a NUL
+        # byte), NOT by UTF-8 validity. A transcript routinely embeds arbitrary subprocess stdout
+        # (latin-1 dumps, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings; gating on strict decode meant
+        # a single stray byte routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch and re-leaked it (the
+        # CV-O-1 fix had turned an always-redact path into a fail-open one). Decode with
+        # errors="replace" so redaction ALWAYS runs on text; only a genuinely binary blob (NUL
+        # present) is copied verbatim.
+        if b"\x00" in raw:
+            data = raw  # genuinely binary — nothing to scrub
         else:
-            data = _scrub(text).encode("utf-8")
+            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
         if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
             pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
         else:
diff --git a/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py b/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py
index f00cd07..01fdff2 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py
@@ -65,7 +65,19 @@ class MlflowExperimentTracker:
         exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
         if exp is not None:
             return str(exp.experiment_id)
-        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
+        # check-then-create is a TOCTOU (#J-004): two concurrent first-time start_run calls (the
+        # 8-thread reasoning pool, or multiple study processes on one shared local store) can both
+        # see None and both create_experiment; MLflow rejects the loser with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS
+        # and the exception would propagate out of start_run, leaving run_id=None and silently
+        # dropping ALL metrics + the provenance artifact for the whole study. On a create race,
+        # re-fetch the now-existing experiment instead of failing.
+        try:
+            return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
+        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize the concurrent-create race to a re-fetch
+            exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
+            if exp is not None:
+                return str(exp.experiment_id)
+            raise
 
     def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
         client = self._client()
diff --git a/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py b/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py
index 79964bf..106212f 100644
--- a/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py
+++ b/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py
@@ -140,10 +140,20 @@ def test_t3_hung_subprocess_is_cancelled_not_leaked(fake_sdk, monkeypatch):
     assert _t.monotonic() - t0 < 5  # cancelled promptly near the 0.3s inner timeout, not hung
 
 
+def _assert_transient(excinfo):
+    # #J-005: RateLimitError and PermanentReasoningError both SUBCLASS ReasoningError, so
+    # `pytest.raises(ReasoningError)` does NOT discriminate a transient mapping from a rate-limit
+    # or permanent one. Assert the exception is the transient class itself, not a decisive subclass.
+    assert not isinstance(excinfo.value, (RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError)), (
+        f"expected a transient ReasoningError, got {type(excinfo.value).__name__}"
+    )
+
+
 def test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient(fake_sdk):
     fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([]))  # no text blocks -> empty
-    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
+    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
         _run()
+    _assert_transient(ei)
 
 
 def test_t3_rate_limit_event_rejected_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
@@ -167,8 +177,9 @@ def test_t3_auth_error_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
 
 def test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
     fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500, errors=["boom"]))
-    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
+    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
         _run()
+    _assert_transient(ei)  # a 500 must be transient, NOT permanent/rate-limit (#J-005)
 
 
 def test_t3_429_not_clobbered_by_trailing_result_error(fake_sdk):
@@ -202,8 +213,9 @@ def test_t3_top_level_rejected_status_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
 def test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
     # the SDK/transport raising (rather than completing the stream) is a transient failure
     fake_sdk.append(RuntimeError("connection reset"))
-    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
+    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
         _run()
+    _assert_transient(ei)  # transport raise must be transient, not permanent/rate-limit (#J-005)
 
 
 def test_t3_missing_auth_fails_loud(monkeypatch, fake_sdk):
diff --git a/tests/test_observability.py b/tests/test_observability.py
index 84831f6..64b6aee 100644
--- a/tests/test_observability.py
+++ b/tests/test_observability.py
@@ -123,6 +123,32 @@ def test_bootstrap_logs_warning_on_setup_failure(monkeypatch, caplog):
     assert any("setup failed" in r.message for r in caplog.records)
 
 
+def test_experiment_id_survives_concurrent_create_race(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
+    # #J-004: check-then-create is a TOCTOU — on the first-time race, create_experiment raises
+    # RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS for the loser. _experiment_id must re-fetch the now-existing
+    # experiment instead of propagating (which would leave run_id=None and drop all metrics).
+    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", f"sqlite:///{tmp_path / 'x.db'}")
+
+    class _Exp:
+        experiment_id = "exp-7"
+
+    class RacingClient:
+        def __init__(self):
+            self.calls = 0
+
+        def get_experiment_by_name(self, name):
+            # first call (the check) sees nothing; after the failed create, the winner's exists
+            self.calls += 1
+            return None if self.calls == 1 else _Exp()
+
+        def create_experiment(self, name):
+            raise RuntimeError("RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS: another writer created it")
+
+    t = MlflowExperimentTracker()
+    client = RacingClient()
+    assert t._experiment_id(client) == "exp-7"  # re-fetched, not raised
+
+
 def test_start_run_terminates_run_when_param_logging_fails(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
     # #F-023: if create_run succeeds but log_param fails, the run must be terminated FAILED, not
     # left orphaned in RUNNING state. Mock the MLflow client to exercise the failure path without
diff --git a/tests/test_scripts.py b/tests/test_scripts.py
index 510b00f..b66eceb 100644
--- a/tests/test_scripts.py
+++ b/tests/test_scripts.py
@@ -123,6 +123,39 @@ def test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim(tmp_path):
     assert dst.read_bytes() == b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary"
 
 
+def test_scrub_fails_closed_on_invalid_utf8_bytes():
+    # #J-001: a stray non-UTF-8 byte must NOT disable redaction for the whole file. The scrubber
+    # decodes errors='replace' and classifies binary by a NUL byte, so a secret next to an invalid
+    # byte is still scrubbed (the pass-6 strict-decode fix had made this fail OPEN).
+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_failclosed")
+    raw = b'{"out":"[REDACTED] caf\xe9 /Users/[REDACTED]/x"}'  # invalid \xe9
+    text = raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
+    out = cap._scrub(text)
+    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out  # secret scrubbed despite the bad byte
+    assert "somebody" not in out
+
+
+def test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_file_with_invalid_utf8(tmp_path):
+    # #J-001 end-to-end: a .jsonl with an invalid byte is still scrubbed on copy, not copied raw.
+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_fc2")
+    src = tmp_path / "t.jsonl"
+    src.write_bytes(b'{"k":"[REDACTED] \x80 end"}')  # invalid \x80
+    dst = tmp_path / "out" / "t.jsonl"
+    cap._copy_scrubbed(src, dst)
+    out = dst.read_bytes()
+    assert b"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv" not in out and b"[REDACTED]" in out
+
+
+def test_scrub_redacts_operator_display_name(monkeypatch):
+    # #J-002: the operator's full display name (from ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES / git user.name) is redacted,
+    # not just the email — distinct PII that leaked beside redacted emails.
+    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "Ada Lovelace")
+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_name")
+    out = cap._scrub("Author: Ada Lovelace <[REDACTED]>; also Ada   Lovelace in prose")
+    assert "Ada Lovelace" not in out and "Ada   Lovelace" not in out  # flexible whitespace too
+    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out  # email still scrubbed
+
+
 def test_example_uses_no_hardcoded_hmac_secret():
     # #F-048: the example must not carry a shippable, publicly-known HMAC secret a user could copy
     # into production. It derives a fresh random secret per run instead.
diff --git a/tests/test_supply_chain.py b/tests/test_supply_chain.py
index e55616d..b53fa96 100644
--- a/tests/test_supply_chain.py
+++ b/tests/test_supply_chain.py
@@ -314,6 +314,28 @@ def test_ci_integration_verifies_services_are_reachable():
     assert "pg_isready" in text and "/readyz" in text
 
 
+def test_ci_pins_build_backend_and_disables_live_build_isolation():
+    # #J-003: the editable install must use the hashed build backend (requirements-build.lock) with
+    # --no-build-isolation, so hatchling isn't fetched live/un-hashed from PyPI (an unscanned,
+    # non-reproducible build-time code-exec window).
+    text = _ci_text()
+    assert "--no-build-isolation --no-deps -e ." in text, "build isolation still live (#J-003)"
+    assert "requirements-build.lock" in text
+    assert (_ROOT / "requirements-build.lock").exists()
+    build = (_ROOT / "requirements-build.lock").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+    assert "hatchling==" in build and "--hash=sha256:" in build
+    assert "pip install --no-deps -e .\n" not in text  # the old live-isolation install is gone
+
+
+def test_ci_license_gate_scopes_to_dependency_set():
+    # #J-006: the license gate must scan the dependency set, not entire site-packages
+    # (--with-system pulls system tooling that can spuriously fail or mask the gate).
+    text = _ci_text()
+    assert "pip-licenses --format=json --with-system" not in text, (
+        "license gate still --with-system"
+    )
+
+
 def test_github_actions_are_sha_pinned():
     # #F-037: every `uses:` third-party action must be pinned to a 40-char commit SHA (a mutable
     # tag can be force-pushed to malicious code). A version tag is allowed only as a comment.

content.content:
1	name: ci
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [main]
6	  pull_request:
7	
8	# Least privilege (#H-008): the default GITHUB_TOKEN can be repo-write; this workflow only reads
9	# the code and uploads artifacts, so pin the token to read-only at the top level. Any job needing
10	# more must opt in explicitly with its own `permissions:`.
11	permissions:
12	  contents: read
13	
14	jobs:
15	  # Dependency-free core: the engine must lint, type-check, and test with NO extras installed
16	  # (optional backends are import-guarded; integration tests skip). This guards ADR-0006.
17	  core:
18	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
19	    strategy:
20	      matrix:
21	        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12", "3.13", "3.14"]
22	    steps:
23	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
24	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
25	        with:
26	          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
27	      - name: Install (no extras, hash-pinned)
28	        run: |
29	          # Install the dev toolchain from the hashed, audited core lockfile so tests run against
30	          # EXACTLY the versions pip-audit scanned and the SBOM describes — not a live PyPI
31	          # resolution where a malicious patch release could execute under test (#F-004). The
32	          # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
33	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
34	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
35	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
36	          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
37	      - name: Lint (ruff)
38	        run: ruff check src tests
39	      - name: Format check (ruff)
40	        run: ruff format --check src tests
41	      - name: Type-check (mypy)
42	        run: mypy
43	      - name: Test (pytest)
44	        run: pytest -q
45	
46	  # Extras lane: install the real backends and run the integration suite so the backend code
47	  # paths actually execute, and mypy type-checks against the REAL (not ignored) library types.
48	  # Service-dependent tests (ollama/qdrant/postgres) skip when the service is absent in CI; the
49	  # extra-only tests (langgraph graph, mlflow lifecycle, OTel, numpy primitives, methodology
50	  # e2e) run for real.
51	  integration:
52	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
53	    services:
54	      postgres:
55	        image: postgres:16
56	        env:
57	          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
58	          POSTGRES_DB: assay
59	        ports: ["5432:5432"]
60	        options: >-
61	          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U postgres"
62	          --health-interval 5s --health-timeout 5s --health-retries 10
63	      qdrant:
64	        image: qdrant/qdrant:v1.12.4
65	        ports: ["6333:6333"]
66	    env:
67	      # loopback host keeps the data-sovereignty guard satisfied; creds are redacted on error
68	      ASSAY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL: postgresql://[REDACTED]@localhost:5432/assay
69	    steps:
70	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
71	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
72	        with:
73	          python-version: "3.12"
74	      - name: Install (all extras, hash-pinned)
75	        run: |
76	          # Install from the hashed all-extras lockfile so the integration suite executes against
77	          # exactly the audited, pinned versions — closing the window where a compromised patch
78	          # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
79	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
80	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
81	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
82	          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
83	      - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
84	        run: mypy
85	      - name: Verify service containers are reachable (no silent skip)
86	        # #H-009: the live Postgres/Qdrant tests SKIP when the service is unreachable, so a
87	        # misconfigured/never-started container would let the integration job report GREEN without
88	        # actually exercising the backends. Fail loudly here if either service isn't up, so the
89	        # gate cannot pass vacuously.
90	        run: |
91	          set -euo pipefail
92	          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
93	            pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432 -U postgres && break || sleep 2
94	            [ "$i" = "30" ] && { echo "::error::Postgres service never became ready"; exit 1; }
95	          done
96	          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
97	            curl -fsS http://localhost:6333/readyz && break || sleep 2
98	            [ "$i" = "30" ] && { echo "::error::Qdrant service never became ready"; exit 1; }
99	          done
100	          echo "postgres + qdrant reachable"
101	      # Real backends now run: langgraph graph + interrupt/resume, mlflow lifecycle, OTel,
102	      # numpy primitives, the T3 drain/classification (synthetic SDK, no metered call), AND —
103	      # via the service containers — the live Postgres checkpointer + Qdrant vector store.
104	      # Ollama and the metered frontier tier are not in CI and skip honestly.
105	      - name: Integration + unit tests with coverage
106	        run: pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing
107	
108	  # Supply-chain: fail on known-vulnerable dependencies (a security/privacy engine must not ship
109	  # with advisories). The scan runs against the committed, fully-pinned, hashed lockfile
110	  # (requirements.lock) so the audit is reproducible across runs and matches what installs
111	  # (#108, #130, #131, #132, #145, #146). An allowlist file (.pip-audit-ignore, one advisory id
112	  # per line, '#' comments and blank lines allowed) covers accepted/unfixable ones.
113	  audit:
114	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
115	    steps:
116	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
117	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
118	        with:
119	          python-version: "3.12"
120	      - name: Install pip-audit
121	        # Pin the audit tooling to exact versions (#G-022): an unpinned pip-audit can change
122	        # advisory results / exit behavior between releases, making the supply-chain gate
123	        # non-reproducible (a green commit could fail tomorrow on a tooling bump) — the analogue
124	        # of the uv pin (#F-031).
125	        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
126	      - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
127	        run: |
128	          # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
129	          # `uv` upgrade can change output format/ordering/annotations and produce spurious diffs
130	          # that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current.
131	          python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
132	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
133	          # ignore the auto-generated header (timestamp/command line) when diffing
134	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
135	            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock"; exit 1; }
136	          # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
137	          # stay in sync too.
138	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
139	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
140	            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
141	          # the build-backend lockfile (hatchling etc.) backs the hash-pinned --no-build-isolation
142	          # editable install (#J-003) and must stay in sync.
143	          printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in
144	          uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock
145	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-build.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock) \
146	            || { echo "::error::requirements-build.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile <(printf 'hatchling\\neditables\\n') --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-build.lock"; exit 1; }
147	      - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
148	        run: |
149	          set -euo pipefail
150	          # --strict: any dependency that cannot be collected/audited FAILS the gate (no silent
151	          # skips). --require-hashes: every pin must carry a hash. Build --ignore-vuln args as an
152	          # array so allowlist entries can't word-split or glob-expand, and read the file
153	          # newline-agnostically so the last line is honored even without a trailing newline.
154	          IGNORES=()
155	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
156	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
157	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
158	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
159	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
160	          fi
161	          pip-audit --strict --require-hashes --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock "${IGNORES[@]}"
162	      - name: SBOM (CycloneDX) from the lockfile
163	        run: |
164	          set -euo pipefail
165	          # Emit a machine-readable software bill of materials for the pinned dependency set so
166	          # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147). No
167	          # `|| true` mask (#F-012): a pip-audit failure must fail the step, and the SBOM must be
168	          # non-empty, so a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
169	          # Apply the SAME allowlist as the gate (#G-015): pip-audit exits non-zero on ANY finding
170	          # regardless of output format, so without the allowlist an ACCEPTED advisory would fail
171	          # SBOM generation here even though the gate step passed — coupling SBOM emission to vuln
172	          # status and making the allowlist ineffective. The gate step already ran first, so a
173	          # NON-allowlisted advisory has failed the job before we reach here.
174	          IGNORES=()
175	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
176	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
177	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
178	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
179	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
180	          fi
181	          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"
182	          test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json
183	          echo "SBOM emitted ($(wc -c < sbom.cyclonedx.json) bytes)"
184	      - name: Upload SBOM artifact
185	        if: always()
186	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
187	        with:
188	          name: sbom-cyclonedx
189	          path: sbom.cyclonedx.json
190	          if-no-files-found: error
191	      - name: License gate (deny strong-copyleft in the pinned dependency set)
192	        run: |
193	          set -euo pipefail
194	          # Fail if any dependency carries a strong-copyleft license incompatible with the
195	          # Apache-2.0 distribution (#147). Scan the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a
196	          # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
197	          # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
198	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
199	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
200	          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
201	          python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
202	          pip-licenses --format=json > licenses.json  # scan the dep set, not system tooling (#J-006)
203	          python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
204	      - name: Upload license report
205	        if: always()
206	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
207	        with:
208	          name: license-report
209	          path: licenses.json
210	          if-no-files-found: warn
211	
212	  # Single required status check (#F-014): branch protection should require ONLY `all-checks`.
213	  # It fans in every other job, so adding a new job automatically gates merges without anyone
214	  # remembering to update the branch-protection rule — and `audit`/`integration` can never be
215	  # silently dropped from the merge gate by requiring just `core`.
216	  all-checks:
217	    if: always()
218	    needs: [core, integration, audit]
219	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
220	    steps:
221	      - name: Verify all required jobs succeeded
222	        run: |
223	          results="${{ needs.core.result }} ${{ needs.integration.result }} ${{ needs.audit.result }}"
224	          echo "job results: $results"
225	          for r in $results; do
226	            if [ "$r" != "success" ]; then
227	              echo "::error::a required job did not succeed ($r)"
228	              exit 1
229	            fi
230	          done
231	          echo "all required jobs succeeded"

content.text: Build backend is hatchling, matching the lock. Now run the test and verify discrimination.

content.text:
The revert simulation confirms discrimination: reverting kills the `--no-build-isolation` assertion (first assert fails) and restores the old `pip install --no-deps -e .\n` line (last assert fails). Two assertions independently fail on revert.

Note: `requirements-build.lock` still appears in reverted text because the reproducibility-guard block in the audit job references it — so that assertion alone wouldn't discriminate, but it's not relied upon. Let me verify the lock file is actually consistent (hatchling version is a real published version, hashes present) and check whether there's a residual gap with `pip` itself being un-isolated.

packaging==26.2
pathspec==1.1.1
pluggy==1.6.0

content.text:
The build lock is complete: hatchling + editables + all transitive (packaging, pathspec, pluggy, trove-classifiers), all hashed. With `--no-build-isolation`, pip uses the already-installed (hash-verified) build backend rather than fetching a fresh isolated build env from PyPI.

Let me consider one subtle gap: `--no-build-isolation` means pip builds in the current environment. The build lock is installed via `--require-hashes`, but is it installed BEFORE the core/full lock or after? Order matters for version conflicts. In all lanes, the main lock installs first, then the build lock. Let me check for potential version clobbering of shared transitive deps (e.g., `packaging`, `pluggy` may also be in requirements.lock).

content.text:
Shared transitive deps are pinned to identical versions across all locks, so installing the build lock after the main lock causes no version clobbering or `--require-hashes` reinstall mismatch. The fix is internally consistent.

Now let me check the reproducibility guard's regen command for correctness. It uses `printf 'hatchling\neditables\n'` as input, matching the header comment in the lock. Let me verify the regen would actually reproduce the committed lock (the guard would catch staleness, but does it reflect what was committed) and check the `--universal` flag consistency.

The lock header says `uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-build.lock` and the guard uses exactly that. Consistent.

One residual consideration: the guard runs `uv pip compile` which resolves `hatchling`/`editables` LIVE against current PyPI at audit time. If a new hatchling release appears, the guard diff fails the build — that's intended (forces a deliberate lock regen). That's acceptable hardening behavior, not a gap.

Let me confirm the test file's `_ci_text` reads the real CI file and the assertions match exactly.

52	def test_pip_audit_runs_strict_and_require_hashes():
53	    # #130: without --strict an unauditable dependency is silently skipped while CI stays green.
54	    # --require-hashes ties the scan to the pinned, hashed lockfile.
55	    text = _ci_text()

content.text:
Confirmed: `_ci_text` reads the real `.github/workflows/ci.yml`. The test asserts against the actual CI file, not a fixture.

All checks pass. Let me summarize my assessment.

Root cause: default build isolation made `pip install -e .` fetch hatchling live and un-hashed from PyPI during build — an unscanned, non-reproducible code-execution window. The fix addresses this at the correct layer: a hashed `requirements-build.lock` (hatchling + editables + full transitive closure, all `--hash` pinned) installed via `--require-hashes`, then `pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .` so pip uses the verified, already-installed backend. Applied to all three lanes (core, integration, license-gate) plus a reproducibility guard. This is the standard, sound approach — not a band-aid.

Discrimination: verified by simulated revert — reverting to `pip install --no-deps -e .` makes the `--no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .` assertion fail AND restores the old `pip install --no-deps -e .\n` line that the negative assertion forbids. Two independent assertions fail on revert.

input.rationale: The fix addresses the real root cause at the correct layer. The vulnerability was default build isolation causing `pip install -e .` to fetch the hatchling build backend live and un-hashed from PyPI (unscanned, non-reproducible build-time code execution). The fix introduces requirements-build.lock containing hatchling==1.30.1, editables==0.6, and the complete transitive closure (packaging, pathspec, pluggy, trove-classifiers) all with sha256 hashes (12 hashes total), installs it with --require-hashes, then runs `pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .` so pip uses the already-installed, hash-verified backend instead of building an isolated env from live PyPI. Applied consistently to all three lanes (core, integration, license-gate) plus a reproducibility-guard diff in the audit job. Shared transitive deps (packaging, pluggy, pathspec) are pinned to identical versions across requirements.lock, requirements-core.lock, and requirements-build.lock, so installing the build lock after the main lock causes no version clobbering or --require-hashes conflict. Build backend in pyproject.toml is `hatchling.build`, matching the lock. The regression test reads the real CI file (_ci_text -> .github/workflows/ci.yml) and the committed requirements-build.lock. Test passes. Revert-discrimination verified by simulation: reverting to `pip install --no-deps -e .` causes the `--no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .` assertion to fail AND the negative `pip install --no-deps -e .\n` assertion to fire — two independent assertions discriminate.
concerns: Minor (not a defect): the `requirements-build.lock in text` assertion alone does NOT discriminate a revert, because that filename still appears in the audit-job reproducibility guard even after reverting the install lines. Discrimination is fully carried by the other two assertions, so the test is still sound, but that one line is non-load-bearing.

Residual (by-design, not a gap): the reproducibility guard runs `uv pip compile` resolving hatchling/editables live against PyPI at audit time, so a new hatchling release will fail the diff and force a deliberate lock regen — intended hardening behavior, not a leak, since the actual build still uses the pinned hashed lock.

content.text:
Review complete. Verdict: CONFIRMED for finding J-003.

The fix correctly closes the build-time live/un-hashed hatchling fetch window via a hashed requirements-build.lock (full transitive closure, 12 sha256 hashes) installed with --require-hashes plus `--no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .` across all three lanes, with a reproducibility guard. Shared transitive versions are consistent across all locks (no clobbering). The regression test reads the real CI file and discriminates on revert via two independent assertions. Two minor, non-defect notes captured in concerns.

Relevant files:
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.github/workflows/ci.yml
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/requirements-build.lock
- /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_supply_chain.py
